_comment: v6 = 1001-doc / 97-query ultimate-scale benchmark. Measured live 2026-05-27 on HeuristicReader + DeterministicEmbedder(128) + HnswIndex. Two backends compared: Claude Opus 4.7 (subprocess) vs MiniMax-M2.5 (HTTP) + Claude fallback.

claude_opus._note: Pure Claude Opus 4.7 via local claude.cmd subprocess. Ingested 2026-05-26.

minimax_m25_with_claude_fallback._note: MiniMax-M2.5 primary + Claude fallback on empty/error. Ingested 2026-05-27.

summary._comment: Switching default ingest backend from Claude to MiniMax+fallback for SAGE bulk-ingest workloads.
summary.verdict: MiniMax wins on cost / speed / ingest robustness; Claude retains a small quality edge. Default to MiniMax for bulk ingest, reserve Claude for quality-critical eval / reasoning.

regression_thresholds._comment: If a future MiniMax run drops below these on the same query set, something broke.
